key_insight: HACO, a human-AI co-discovery system, found that a masked generative model from vision (MaskGIT) can be transferred and adapted into a strong crystal structure prediction framework.
engineering_relevance: The work suggests AI co-scientists can accelerate materials algorithm design by combining cross-domain model search with lightweight human guidance to improve crystal structure prediction.
method: Searched across generative modeling ideas from multiple fields instead of only CSP literature
Instantiated crystal structures as discrete tokens in a masked generative transformer
Added symmetry tokens, space-group-stratified sampling, and sub-bin coordinate refinement to form MaskGXT
evidence_signal: On the MP-20 polymorph split, MaskGXT achieved 79.06% METRe accuracy versus 70.87% for the strongest baseline, and also led on standard MP-20 and MPTS-52 benchmarks.
watch_item: Performance may depend on benchmark-specific assumptions and on cheap, fast validation loops that make sparse human steering practical.
paper_title: Discovering Crystal Structure Prediction Algorithms with an AI Co-Scientist
